title: Housing Near the Cleveland Clinic for Traveling Medical Staff
metaTitle: Housing Near Cleveland Clinic for Medical Staff
metaDescription: A commute-focused guide to housing near Cleveland Clinic's main campus for traveling medical staff — drive times, transit, and how to tell us what your team needs.
secondaryKeywords: rentals near cleveland clinic main campus
answerBox: Cleveland Clinic's main campus sits at East 93rd and Euclid Avenue. Most short-term medical staff prioritize a short, predictable commute over a specific neighborhood. Homes on Cleveland's East and Southeast side put you within roughly a 5-to-20-minute drive, and the HealthLine bus runs directly down Euclid. Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team — tell us your assignment location and dates and we'll see what we can help with.

sections.h2: Where the main campus actually is
body: Cleveland Clinic's main campus is centered near **East 93rd Street and Euclid Avenue**, just southeast of downtown. If your assignment is at a satellite location — Fairview, Hillcrest, Marymount, or one of the family health centers — the commute math changes, so it helps to confirm your exact building before you fix on an area.

We frame housing here by **how you get to work**, not by who lives where. What matters for a traveling assignment is a drive or transit route you can count on at shift-change hours, parking that works for your schedule, and a lease term that lines up with your contract.

sections.h2: Thinking in drive times, not neighborhoods
body: For a 12-week or open-ended assignment, a reliable commute usually beats everything else. Cleveland's **East and Southeast side** — where most of the homes we work with are concentrated — generally sits within a short drive of the main campus, and the highway grid (I-90, I-77, and I-490) gives you more than one way in when one route backs up.
A few practical things to weigh when you compare options:

items: Off-peak vs. shift-change drive time — a 10-minute midday trip can double at 7 a.m.
Whether the home has off-street parking, which matters most for night and rotating shifts
Proximity to a highway on-ramp rather than surface streets end to end
How close you are to a HealthLine stop if you'd rather not drive daily

sections.h2: Transit: the HealthLine runs to your door
body: The **RTA HealthLine** is a bus rapid transit route that runs the length of **Euclid Avenue**, connecting downtown and Public Square directly to the Cleveland Clinic and University Circle. For staff who would rather skip a car and the parking cost, living within walking distance of a HealthLine stop can turn the commute into a single, predictable ride.

Coverage thins out as you move away from the Euclid corridor, so if car-free living is a priority, tell us up front — it narrows the search in a useful way rather than a limiting one. You can browse the kinds of homes we work with on our [Cleveland rentals](/cleveland-rentals/) page to get a feel for what's typical.

sections.h2: How lease terms and vouchers fit a travel assignment
body: Every home we work with **welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8)** and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. That's worth flagging because some traveling staff — or family members relocating with them — hold a voucher and assume it limits their options near a hospital. It doesn't have to. If that applies to you, our [Section 8 housing in Cleveland](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) guide walks through how it works locally.

Be aware of one honest caveat on lease length: standard leases are typically annual, and Cleveland itself has **no source-of-income ordinance** (only Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale do). We don't keep a furnished or month-to-month inventory on the shelf, so if your contract is short, tell us the exact dates and we'll be straight with you about what's realistic rather than promising something we can't deliver.

We're a **local rental team**, not a national relocation vendor — we help renters find, tour, and apply for homes we work with across Greater Cleveland, with about 90+ homes concentrated on the East and Southeast side plus some suburbs and the Akron/Lorain/Elyria area. The fastest way to get real answers is to send us the specifics.

Share your campus or building, your start and end dates, bedrooms and household size, and whether you'll drive or rely on the HealthLine. We'll tell you honestly what in our current selection fits — and where it doesn't. Reach us at **(440) 444-4737** or **support@rentfindercleveland.com**, book a tour on our [showing page](/p/book-showing), or send your team's needs through [corporate leasing](/corporate-leasing/).

faq.q: How far is the housing you work with from Cleveland Clinic's main campus?
faq.a: Most of the homes we work with are on Cleveland's East and Southeast side, generally within a short drive of East 93rd and Euclid. Exact drive time depends on your building and shift hours, so we compare routes with you based on your specific assignment.

faq.q: Can I get to the Clinic without a car?
faq.a: Often yes. The RTA HealthLine bus runs straight down Euclid Avenue to the main campus and University Circle. If car-free living matters to you, tell us and we'll focus the search near a HealthLine stop.

faq.q: Do you offer furnished or short-term housing for travel nurses?
faq.a: We don't keep furnished or month-to-month units on hand — we're a local rental team working mostly with standard annual leases. Send us your dates and we'll be honest about what's realistic rather than overpromise.

faq.q: I have a Housing Choice Voucher — can I still rent near the Clinic?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with welcomes Section 8 and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. Send us your voucher bedroom size and preferred commute and we'll match it to what's open.

faq.q: How do we set up housing for a whole team?
faq.a: Tell us your team's needs — locations, dates, and how many people — through our corporate leasing page or by phone, and we'll see how we can help. We're a local rental team, not a relocation company, so we'll be direct about what we can and can't cover.
